perturbation.original_text: 12.2.1 material breach or non-compliance by the oth er Party of some obligation, undertaking, representation, warranty or payment contained in th is Agreement, if such default is not remedied within thirty (30) days of receipt of writ ten notice to that effect;
perturbation.changed_text: 12.2.1 material breach or non-compliance by the other Party of some obligation, undertaking, representation, warranty or payment contained in this Agreement, if such default is not remedied within sixty (60) days of receipt of written notice to that effect;
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the time frame to remedy a breach, modifying the duration from thirty (30) days to sixty (60) days. In Section 12.4, it mentions that 'Upon receipt by either Party of a notice of termina tion under this section 13 or expiry of the delay within which defa ult may be cured under section 13.2.1', implying the original remedy period of 30 days is applicable, therefore contradicting the duration mentioned in the new section 12.2.1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.4 EFFECT OF TERMINATION. Upon receipt by either Party of a notice of termina tion under this section 13 or expiry of the delay within which defa ult may be cured under section 13.2.1, McGill will make all reasonable efforts to stop work on th e Project and limit further expense of the Funds, provided that McGill shall have the right to disburse any sum of money committed at the time of termination. Sponsor shall pay to McGill al l expenses reasonably incurred, committed to, or made in relation to the Project up to and includ ing the date of receipt of a notice of termination or expiry of the delay within which def ault could be cured under section 13.2.1, and shall pay for all costs and fees related to the ter mination of the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the cure period for a breach from thirty (30) days to sixty (60) days in section 12.2.1. The contradicted text in section 12.4 refers to the 'expiry of the delay within which default may be cured under section 13.2.1', implicitly assuming the original 30-day cure period. Because of this change in 12.2.1 from 30 to 60 days, 12.4 now has conflicting information on the expiry date, creating a strong contradiction. This follows the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction, where different sections provide conflicting deadlines.

perturbation.original_text: 3.4 PUBLICATION : The Parties are not restricted from presenting, p ublishing or otherwise disseminating the results of any research relating to a Project (including without limitation the Project IP) at symposia, professional meetings or f or academic evaluation or other academic purposes (including, without limitation, publishing thesis, course reports, journal articles or other academic publications) (a “Publication”) provided t hat during the Project and for a period of two years following the completion or other termination of the Project, the Academic Supervisor and the Intern will provide the Sponsor with a copy of any proposed Publication containing the results of any research relating to a Project or any Projec t IP at least thirty (30) days in advance of the proposed publication date.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.4 PUBLICATION : The Parties are not restricted from presenting, publishing or otherwise disseminating the results of any research relating to a Project (including without limitation the Project IP) at symposia, professional meetings or for academic evaluation or other academic purposes (including, without limitation, publishing thesis, course reports, journal articles or other academic publications) (a “Publication”) provided that during the Project and for a period of one year following the completion or other termination of the Project, the Academic Supervisor and the Intern will provide the Sponsor with a copy of any proposed Publication containing the results of any research relating to a Project or any Project IP at least thirty (30) days in advance of the proposed publication date.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the duration for providing advance copies of publications to the Sponsor, changing it from two years to one year following project completion. Section 19, the survival clause, states that Section 3.4 (Publication) survives the end or termination of the Project. This implies that the original agreement of two years should still be upheld, contradicting the modified one-year period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 19. SURVIVAL : Notwithstanding the foregoing, the provisions of Section 3.2 (the academic and research license); Section 3.4 (Publication); Secti on 4 (Indemnification); Section 7 (Non-Disclosure); and Section 11 (Warranties) and will survive the end or other termination of the Project.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 3.4, reducing the period for providing advance copies of publications to the Sponsor from two years to one year after project completion. Section 19, the survival clause, explicitly states that Section 3.4 survives the end or termination of the Project. The original agreement of two years is contradicted by the modified one-year period, because Section 19 is supposed to uphold the terms within section 3.4, but Section 3.4 was modified, resulting in an inconsistency and contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. DESIGNATED REPRESENTATIVES : Each party designates a representative for coordi nating receipt, release and delivery of Confidential Information, w hich for McGill will be Intern AND Name of Academic Supervisor and/or Office of Sponsored Research repr esentative and for Sponsor: Name of designated representative for Sponsor, or another individual(s ) as the Party may designate in writing to the othe r Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. DESIGNATED REPRESENTATIVES : Each party designates a representative for coordinating receipt, release and delivery of Confidential Information, which for McGill will be Intern OR Name of Academic Supervisor and/or Office of Sponsored Research representative and for Sponsor: Name of designated representative for Sponsor, or another individual(s) as the Party may designate in writing to the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the requirement for McGill's designated representatives from "Intern AND Name of Academic Supervisor" to "Intern OR Name of Academic Supervisor." Previously, McGill was required to have both the intern AND the Academic Supervisor to coordinate the receipt, release and delivery of confidential information. By changing "AND" to "OR", it creates a scenario where McGill only needs one of them, contradicting section 7.1 Recipient must keep Confidential Information in confidence. Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information only to such of its employees, directors, officers, agents, consultants and administrators who have a need-to-know such information for the Project provided that they are advised of the confidential nature of the Confident ial Information and are under an obligation to maintain its confidentiality, which implies that both the intern AND academic supervisor are a need to know of such information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.1 Recipient must keep Confidential Information in confidence. Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information only to such of its employ ees, directors, officers, agents, consultants and administrators who have a need-to-know such informa tion for the Project provided that they are advised of the confidential nature of the Confident ial Information and are under an obligation to maintain its confidentiality. Recipient must not ot herwise disclose Confidential Information to any person or third party without the prior written app roval of the Discloser.
perturbation.justification: The change from "AND" to "OR" in the Designated Representatives section directly contradicts the need-to-know requirement in section 7.1. Originally, both the intern and the Academic Supervisor were required to coordinate, implying both had a need to know confidential information. The change allows for only one to be designated, conflicting with the requirement in 7.1 that employees/agents who have access to the Confidential Information have a "need-to-know" such information for the Project, implying both are needed. Since now only one could be designated, it creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 16. RELATIONSHIP OF THE PARTIES : During the course of the Project, the Intern rem ains a student/PDF of McGill. Moreover the Intern is not a n employee, agent or representative of McGill. The Intern does not become an employee of the Sponsor a nd will not sign any agreements with the Sponsor. Nothing in this Agreement shall make one Party the partner of another Party nor constitute one Party t he agent or legal representative of another Party, or create any fiduciary relationship between them.
perturbation.changed_text: 16. RELATIONSHIP OF THE PARTIES : During the course of the Project, the Intern rem ains a student/PDF of McGill. Moreover the Intern may be an employee, agent or representative of McGill. The Intern does not become an employee of the Sponsor a nd will not sign any agreements with the Sponsor. Nothing in this Agreement shall make one Party the partner of another Party nor constitute one Party t he agent or legal representative of another Party, or create any fiduciary relationship between them.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating the 'Intern may be an employee, agent or representative of McGill', where in the original text it mentioned 'the Intern is not an employee, agent or representative of McGill'. This in-text contradiction can cause legal uncertainty on what is the correct relationship between McGill and the intern.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 16. RELATIONSHIP OF THE PARTIES : During the course of the Project, the Intern rem ains a student/PDF of McGill. Moreover the Intern may be an employee, agent or representative of McGill. The Intern does not become an employee of the Sponsor a nd will not sign any agreements with the Sponsor. Nothing in this Agreement shall make one Party the partner of another Party nor constitute one Party t he agent or legal representative of another Party, or create any fiduciary relationship between them.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 16 to state that the 'Intern may be an employee, agent or representative of McGill,' which directly contradicts the original text's statement that 'the Intern is not an employee, agent or representative of McGill.' The contradicted text is in the same location as the Changed Text. The change introduces a clear inconsistency regarding the intern's relationship with McGill, thus creating an in-text contradiction as defined in the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation definition.
